headline: Apollo TAKKION Exit Resets Renewable Exposure And Undervalued Share Story
summary: Apollo Global Management agreed to sell a majority stake in renewable energy services provider TAKKION to private equity firm Siris. The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2026, subject to customary approvals and closing conditions. The deal reflects Apollo's active portfolio management and a shift in how it is positioned within the renewable energy ecosystem. Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO), recently trading at $108.14, sits at a notable point for investors following its mix of...

headline: Apollo to Launch First Long-Term Asset Fund in the UK
summary: CG Apollo Global Diversified Credit LTAF to serve UK Defined Contribution pension schemesLONDON, March 10, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Apollo (NYSE: APO) today announced that it is set to launch the CG Apollo Global Diversified Credit LTAF (the “LTAF” or the “Fund”), a multi-sector private credit solution, after the Fund recently received FCA authorization.
